sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me check the segmentation to see all objects
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Group nodes by color and location
print(f"Total nodes: {len(nodes)}")

# Find nodes in different regions
left_panel = []
right_panel = []
buttons = []
boxes = []
trigger = []

for node in nodes:
    # Get approximate center of the node
    boundary = node['boundary']
    if boundary:
        avg_row = sum([p[0] for p in boundary]) / len(boundary)
        avg_col = sum([p[1] for p in boundary]) / len(boundary)
    else:
        avg_row, avg_col = 0, 0
    
    if avg_col < 30:
        if avg_row > 50:
            boxes.append(node)
        else:
            left_panel.append(node)
    elif avg_col < 62:
        if avg_row > 32 and avg_row < 60:
            buttons.append(node)
        elif avg_row > 50:
            trigger.append(node)
        else:
            right_panel.append(node)

print(f"\nLeft panel nodes: {len(left_panel)}")
print(f"Right panel nodes: {len(right_panel)}")
print(f"Button nodes: {len(buttons)}")
print(f"Box nodes: {len(boxes)}")
print(f"Trigger nodes: {len(trigger)}")

# Print box details
for i, box in enumerate(boxes):
    boundary = box['boundary']
    if boundary:
        avg_row = sum([p[0] for p in boundary]) / len(boundary)
        avg_col = sum([p[1] for p in boundary]) / len(boundary)
        print(f"  Box {i}: color={box['color']}, pixels={box['pixels']}, center=({avg_row:.1f}, {avg_col:.1f})")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
